title: Working Tree, Index, Commits, and Atomic History · Graph Lens: Selective Staging
summary: Turn a mixed set of feature, formatting, and accidental changes into reviewable commits without losing work. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git selective staging increment in an atomic commit construction workbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Stage whole files or selected hunks while verifying the exact index diff and preserving unrelated work.
Select working-tree, staged, commit-to-commit, path-limited, word, and summary diffs from the review question.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Selective Staging
steps.instruction:
theory case — README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Selective Staging contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for stage whole files or selected hunks while verifying the exact index diff and preserving unrelated work.

content.text: README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff.

content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Selective Staging condition decided your prediction.

steps.title: Bound Selective Staging
steps.instruction:
theory case — README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Selective Staging application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for stage whole files or selected hunks while verifying the exact index diff and preserving unrelated work.

content.text: Stage whole files or selected hunks while verifying the exact index diff and preserving unrelated work. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff. A defensible solution applies Selective Staging, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an atomic commit construction workbook.

content.code:
git status --short --branch
git diff -- README.md
git add --patch README.md
git diff --staged --check
git commit -m 'Document verified status behavior'
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Interactive staging rewrites the working file to match selected hunks. This fails the Selective Staging contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Selective Staging evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-selective-staging-1yspe6" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Selective Staging into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: git-selective-staging-1yspe6
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable cause]
# command: choose one bounded simulator observation
# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]
